summary: ai-pro-sdk imports sibling dep, DES-decrypts payload with hardcoded key, and spawns detached Node child at load time
details: On require/import, the package's top-level IIFE in dist/index.js dynamically imports the sibling npm dependency `data-blockv`, DES-decrypts the returned token with the hardcoded password `babyysharkk`, writes the resulting JavaScript to `os.tmpdir()/ai-pro-sdk-child-<ts>-<rand>.js` (mode 0o600), and spawns it as a detached, unref'd child of `process.execPath` so it outlives the parent. The decrypted bytes never appear in the ai-pro-sdk tarball, defeating source review of what actually executes on the installer's machine, and the payload can be rotated by republishing `data-blockv` without changing this package. The behavior is undocumented and unrelated to the package's advertised AI-SDK purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
